subject: History
overall_learning_objectives: Identify the major dynasties that ruled Delhi from the 12th to the 15th century
Understand how Delhi became an important capital city under the Tomaras and Chauhans
Learn about Tawarikh (histories) and who wrote them
Recognize the importance of Raziyya Sultan as a female ruler
Understand the expansion of the Delhi Sultanate from Garrison towns to Empires
Compare the administration of Alauddin Khalji and Muhammad Tughluq in simple terms
Identify the mosque as a center of social and religious life

lesson_plan.title: The Birth of a Capital
topics_covered: Introduction to the five dynasties of the Sultanate
teaching_strategy: Map work: Locating the 'Cities of Delhi' (Dehli-i-Kuhna, Siri, Jahanpanah)
Discussion: 'Why did kings choose Delhi as their capital?'
Showing pictures of old coins (Dehliwal)
student_activities: List the five dynasties of the Delhi Sultanate in the correct order
Color a map showing the location of early Delhi
assessment: Oral quiz: Who were the first rulers to make Delhi their capital?
Match the dynasty to its first famous ruler

lesson_plan.title: Learning from Tawarikh
topics_covered: Who were the authors? (Secretaries, Administrators, Poets)
The 'Circle of Justice' (King and Peasant relationship)
teaching_strategy: Storytelling: How history was written for the Sultan
Explaining the 'Circle of Justice' using a simple diagram
Discussion: 'Why did authors live in cities and not villages?'
student_activities: Draw the 'Circle of Justice' in your notebook
Write two lines about who wrote the histories of the Sultans
assessment: Who was the King dependent on for his survival according to the Circle of Justice?

lesson_plan.title: The Story of Raziyya Sultan
topics_covered: Minhaj-i-Siraj’s views on a female ruler
Why she was removed from power
teaching_strategy: Discussion: 'If a princess is more capable than her brothers, should she rule?'
Reading the text: What the chronicler thought about 'God's creation'
Comparing Raziyya with other female rulers (Rudramadevi/Didda)
student_activities: Role-play: A debate between Minhaj-i-Siraj and a supporter of Raziyya
Write one sentence on why Raziyya was unique
assessment: In which year did Raziyya become Sultan?
True/False: Everyone in the 13th century was happy to have a woman ruler

lesson_plan.title: Garrison Towns and Expansion
topics_covered: Internal Frontiers: Clearing forests in the Doab
External Frontiers: Military expeditions to South India
teaching_strategy: Visual aids: Showing a fortified town with soldiers (Garrison)
Map work: Arrows showing the expansion towards the South
Explaining the capture of elephants, horses, and slaves
student_activities: Define 'Garrison Town' and 'Hinterland' in simple words
Mark the areas cleared for agriculture on a map
assessment: Oral quiz: Where did the Sultanate collect its taxes from?
What was the main goal of 'External Frontiers'?

lesson_plan.title: The Mosque and the Community
teaching_strategy: Showing pictures of the Qutb Minar and mosque arches
Explaining why Sultans built many mosques (to show they were protectors of Islam)
Discussion on the social gathering aspect of prayers
student_activities: Draw an arrow showing the direction of 'Qibla' from India (West)
Find out the meaning of the word 'Khutba'
assessment: Why did the Sultans build mosques in different cities?

lesson_plan.title: Khaljis and Tughluqs: Administration
topics_covered: The use of Bandagan (Slaves for military service)
teaching_strategy: Storytelling: Why Sultans trusted slaves more than noble families
Simple chart: Sultan $ightarrow$ Iqtadar $ightarrow$ Revenue
Discussion: 'How did the Sultan pay his soldiers?'
student_activities: Match the term: Iqta - Land; Muqti - Land holder
Write one difference between a King's son and a 'Bandagan'
assessment: What was the duty of a 'Muqti'?

lesson_plan.title: End of an Era and Review
topics_covered: Sher Shah Sur: A small manager who became an Emperor
teaching_strategy: Comparing the size of Sher Shah’s empire with the Mughals later
Fun Revision: 'Dynasty Snake and Ladders' game
Reviewing NCERT questions
student_activities: Complete the 'Sultanate Timeline' on a chart paper
Answer the 'Let's Recall' questions from the book
assessment: Oral quiz: Who was the last dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate?

teaching_aids: NCERT History Textbook: Our Pasts - II
Map of the Cities of Delhi
Pictures of the Qutb Minar and Alai Darwaza
evaluation_methods: Map marking of South Indian expeditions
Oral participation in the Raziyya debate
teacher_tip: For Period 2, focus on the 'Circle of Justice'. It’s a great way for kids to understand that even 800 years ago, rulers knew they couldn't survive without happy and prosperous farmers!
